search_reminder_message: Remember you also have Grep, Read, Glob, prompt hooks, and agent hooks available. Prefer deterministic search before spawning a verifier agent.

regex_rules.title: Ban stdlib logger
regex_rules.message: Stdlib logging is banned in Python sources. Use the project logger abstraction instead.

regex_rules.title: Block broad exception handler
Block silent exception swallow
Ban Python Any
regex_rules.message:
Python `Any` is blocked. Use a concrete type, Protocol, TypedDict, TypeVar, or a narrow union.

Instead of:
    def process(data: Any) -> Any:

Use:
    def process(data: Mapping[str, str]) -> ProcessResult:

For generic containers: list[str], dict[str, int], Sequence[T]
For optional: str | None (not Optional[Any])
For callbacks: Callable[[int, str], bool]
For duck typing: define a Protocol with the methods you actually need.

regex_rules.title: Ban Python type suppressions
regex_rules.message:
Python type or lint suppression comments (# type: ignore, # noqa, # pylint: disable, # pyright: ignore) are blocked. Fix the underlying typing or lint issue instead.

Common fixes:
- `# type: ignore[arg-type]` → fix the argument type or add an overload
- `# type: ignore[return-value]` → narrow the return type or add a TypeGuard
- `# type: ignore[assignment]` → use a Union type or restructure
- `# noqa: E501` → break the line or extract a variable
- If the library has bad stubs, add a local .pyi stub or use a Protocol.

regex_rules.title: Block shell insertion of ignore directives
regex_rules.message: Bash commands that insert JS/TS lint or type suppressions are blocked.

regex_rules.title: Block TS/JS ignore directives
regex_rules.message: JS/TS lint or type suppression comments are blocked. Fix the root cause instead.

regex_rules.title: Block TS any
regex_rules.message: TypeScript `any` is blocked. Use a specific type or generic.

regex_rules.title: Block unsafe TS assertions
regex_rules.message: Unsafe TypeScript assertions (`as any|unknown|never`) are blocked.

regex_rules.title: Block complex inline styles
regex_rules.message: Complex inline styles are blocked. Move them into tokens, classes, or shared style primitives.

regex_rules.title: Block Rust TODO markers
regex_rules.message: Rust TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK markers are blocked. Track follow-up work outside the source file.

regex_rules.title: Block Rust unwrap
regex_rules.message: Rust `unwrap()` is blocked. Use `?`, `expect` with context, or explicit error handling.

regex_rules.title: Search reminder
regex_rules.additional_context: Remember you also have Grep, Read, Glob, prompt hooks, and agent hooks available. Prefer deterministic search before spawning a verifier agent.

regex_rules.title: Block Python source shell edits
regex_rules.message: Shell edits to Python source files under src/ are blocked. Use the Edit or Write tool so size and quality guards apply.

regex_rules.title: Block datetime.now fallback
Block default value swallow
Block silent None return
Block assertion roulette
regex_rules.message:
Three or more consecutive bare assert statements without messages are blocked. Add descriptive failure messages so failures are self-documenting.

Instead of:
    assert result.status == 200
    assert result.body is not None
    assert "email" in result.body

Use:
    assert result.status == 200, f"Expected 200, got {{result.status}}"
    assert result.body is not None, "Response body should not be None"
    assert "email" in result.body, f"Missing email in {{result.body.keys()}}"

Descriptive messages turn a bare `AssertionError` into instant debugging context.

regex_rules.title: Block Python TODO/FIXME markers
regex_rules.message:
Python TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX markers are blocked. Don't leave work-tracking comments in source code.

Instead of:
    # TODO: add retry logic
    # FIXME: this breaks with unicode

Do one of:
• Fix it now (preferred)
• Open a GitHub issue and reference it: # See #1234
• Add it to CLAUDE.md or a project TODO file

If you're actively working on the code, just implement the fix instead of leaving a marker for future-you.

regex_rules.title: Block commented-out code
regex_rules.message: Blocks of commented-out code are blocked. Remove dead code instead of commenting it out.

regex_rules.title: Block hardcoded file paths
regex_rules.message: Hardcoded file paths are blocked. Use environment variables, config, or pathlib for portable path resolution.

regex_rules.title: Block Python magic numbers
regex_rules.message: Magic numbers (large literals) are blocked outside constants/config modules. Define named constants instead.

regex_rules.title: Block Python test smells
regex_rules.message:
Test smells detected. Fix the specific issue:

• time.sleep() → use async wait, polling with timeout, or freeze time:
    await asyncio.wait_for(condition(), timeout=5.0)

• try/except in test → use pytest.raises() as a context manager:
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="invalid email"):
        validate(bad_input)

• pytest.mark.skip() without reason → always provide a reason:
    @pytest.mark.skip(reason="Requires live API credentials")

• unittest-style asserts (assertEqual, assertTrue) → use plain assert:
    assert actual == expected, f"Got {{actual}}"

regex_rules.title: Block test loops/conditionals with asserts
regex_rules.message:
Loops containing assert statements are blocked in tests. Use @pytest.mark.parametrize for data-driven tests instead.

Instead of:
    for x in [1, 2, 3]:
        result = func(x)
        assert result > 0

Use:
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("x", [1, 2, 3])
    def test_func(x):
        result = func(x)
        assert result > 0

For complex iteration with setup, extract a helper and parametrize the inputs.

regex_rules.title: Block fixtures outside conftest
regex_rules.message: Global pytest fixtures must be defined in conftest.py, not individual test files.

regex_rules.title: Block Python linter config edits
regex_rules.message: Edits to Python linter configuration files are blocked. Modify configuration only with explicit approval.

regex_rules.title: Block Python linter config shell edits
regex_rules.message: Shell edits to Python linter config files are blocked.

regex_rules.title: Block frontend linter config edits
regex_rules.message: Edits to frontend linter/formatter configuration files are blocked. Modify with explicit approval.

regex_rules.title: Block frontend linter config shell edits
regex_rules.message: Shell edits to frontend linter/formatter config files are blocked.

regex_rules.title: Block code-quality test edits
regex_rules.message: Direct edits to src/test/code-quality.test.ts are blocked.

regex_rules.title: Block code-quality test bash edits
regex_rules.message: Shell edits to src/test/code-quality.test.ts are blocked.

regex_rules.title: Block tests/quality edits
regex_rules.message: Edits to tests/quality/ (except baselines.json) are blocked.

regex_rules.title: Block tests/quality bash edits
regex_rules.message: Shell edits to tests/quality/ are blocked.

regex_rules.title: Block hardcoded design values
regex_rules.message: Hardcoded typography (font-size, font-weight) and color hex values are blocked outside design token files. Use design tokens or theme variables.

regex_rules.title: Block TS TODO markers
regex_rules.message: JS/TS TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX markers are blocked. Track follow-up work in issues.

regex_rules.title: Block Rust magic numbers
regex_rules.message: Rust magic numbers are blocked outside constants modules. Define named constants.

regex_rules.title: Block shell quality bypasses
regex_rules.message:
Shell quality bypasses are blocked. Handle errors explicitly instead.

• `set +e` → Keep `set -e` and handle expected failures with `if` or `||`:
    if ! command_that_might_fail; then
        echo "Failed, handling gracefully"
    fi

• `2>/dev/null` → Check the exit code and handle the error:
    if output=$(command 2>&1); then
        echo "$output"
    else
        echo "command failed: $output" >&2; exit 1
    fi

• `|| true` / `|| :` → Use `|| {{ handle_error; }}` or document why failure is acceptable.

regex_rules.title: Git pre-check reminder
regex_rules.additional_context: Before committing: verify all tests pass, linters are clean, and no quality regressions are introduced. Run the project quality gate if one exists.

regex_rules.title: Warn on baselines edit
regex_rules.additional_context: WARNING: You are editing baseline/snapshot files. Verify that changes reflect intentional behavior updates, not regressions. Review diffs carefully.

regex_rules.title: Warn on baselines bash edit
regex_rules.additional_context: WARNING: You are editing baseline files via shell. Verify changes reflect intentional behavior updates.

regex_rules.title: Block enforcer config edits
regex_rules.message: Enforcer configuration is read-only. Config edits require human approval outside the agent session.

regex_rules.title: Block enforcer config shell edits
regex_rules.message: Shell edits to enforcer configuration are blocked. Config changes require human approval.

regex_rules.title: git stash is banned
regex_rules.message: git stash is banned. Do not use git stash to prove failures are pre-existing or to temporarily revert changes. If a test fails, fix it or document it.

regex_rules.title: Block underscore-prefixed sibling file pattern
regex_rules.message:
Creating a `_prefix_*.py` sibling file is blocked. When splitting a module, create a sub-package (directory with `__init__.py`) instead.

Instead of:
    agents/_executor_fill.py
    agents/_executor_routing.py
    agents/_executor_types.py

Use:
    agents/executor/__init__.py   (re-exports public API)
    agents/executor/fill.py
    agents/executor/routing.py
    agents/executor/types.py

The __init__.py should re-export so external imports stay the same.

regex_rules.title: Protect test integrity baseline and checker
regex_rules.message: Test integrity infrastructure is protected. The baseline and validation script cannot be modified by agents. Re-baseline requires human approval outside the agent session.

regex_rules.title: Protect test integrity files from shell edits
regex_rules.message: Test integrity infrastructure is protected from shell modification. The baseline and validation script cannot be modified by agents.

regex_rules.title: Remind to use pytest multiprocessing
regex_rules.additional_context:
⚡ Use pytest-xdist for parallel test execution. Add `-n auto` to let xdist pick the optimal worker count:

  pytest tests/ -n auto -v --tb=short

This can cut test suite time by 2-8x on multi-core machines. Only skip -n when you need serial execution (e.g., tests with shared state, --pdb, or coverage collection).
